cuisine_type: 本帮菜 (Shanghainese — 中华老字号 / state-recognized old brand)
address: 上海市黄浦区福州路556号 (relocated to current address in 1997)
restaurant_group: 老正兴 (state-recognized 中华老字号 since the Qing dynasty)
signature_dishes: 草头圈子 (clover-topped pork intestine ring)
vibe_and_space: Historic 中华老字号 — large dining halls, round tables, no pretense. Tourist-friendly during lunch (the Michelin star drove a clear traffic spike after 2020); also serves a loyal local crowd. Tables are tightly packed, ambient noise is high (loud / 闹哄哄 per reviewer), service is described as 'not warm but adequate' — typical 国营 老字号 register.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Founded 1862 — predates almost every other named Shanghainese restaurant. Michelin one-star since 2020 (kept in 2026 list). Recommend for a multi-generation family meal where you want classical Shanghainese without the modern-tasting-menu treatment; signature 草头圈子 and 油爆河虾 are textbook. NOT the place for a date or for diners who care about service warmth or quiet ambience. The Michelin star is best read as 'recognition of tradition' rather than 'recognition of refinement' — see contradictions field below. Best slotted in the lunchtime slot (less queue) and ordered from the classics list (新派 / fusion menu items are weaker per reviewer consensus).
contradictions: Strong Michelin one-star institutional signal (2020-2026 continuous) BUT Tripadvisor ranks it #772 of 8868 Shanghai restaurants with 4.0/5 and 大众点评 reviewers regularly cite: (1) 1+ hour lunch waits even on weekdays, (2) cramped table layout, (3) inconsistent execution — undercooked 油爆虾 shells, hardened 糖醋小排, aged 草头. The contradiction pattern is canonical for Shanghainese 老字号 with Michelin recognition: the star rewards the lineage and signatures, not the day-to-day kitchen consistency. Anti-pattern flagged in proposal.md ('Michelin authority vs. occasion fit') applies here — do not silently inherit the Michelin signal for date / business contexts.
